heading: Duplicates
label: 7501 Duplicates
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)750146:643(h)Section 7501(a) provides that if a license, certificate or document issued to an individual is lost due to a marine casualty, the individual will be supplied with a duplicate without cost.Subsection (b) provides that if a license, certificate or document is lost for any reason other than a marine casualty, the seaman must pay a reasonable cost for the replacement.

Amendments2021—Subsec. (b). substituted “holder” for “seaman”.Pub. L. 116–2831985—Subsec. (a). substituted “certificate of registry, or merchant mariner’s document” for “certificate, or document”.Pub. L. 99–36
amendments: Amendments2021—Subsec. (b). substituted “holder” for “seaman”.Pub. L. 116–2831985—Subsec. (a). substituted “certificate of registry, or merchant mariner’s document” for “certificate, or document”.Pub. L. 99–36

children.label: a
children.text: If a license, certificate of registry, or merchant mariner’s document issued under this part is lost as a result of a marine casualty, the holder shall be supplied with a duplicate without cost.

children.label: b
children.text: For any other loss, the holder may obtain a duplicate on payment of reasonable costs prescribed by regulation by the Secretary.
